$comment: SQLite testfixture-link arc (TF-C39, 2026-07-21): RUN witness for shipped <zlib.h> (zlib.json / D-FFI-ZLIB-DESCRIPTOR), the descriptor that clears sqlite ext/misc/zipfile.c's F001A. A real raw-deflate -> inflate round-trip on libz.so.1 (windowBits -15, as zipfile.c) + a crc32 cross-check returns 42 iff byte-exact. Behavioral layout witness: deflateInit2_/inflateInit2_ reject a wrong (int)sizeof(z_stream) (Z_VERSION_ERROR -> returns 1), and deflate/inflate read/write next_in/avail_in/next_out/avail_out/total_out at the ABI offsets (a wrong offset -> returns 6/7/8, never 42). RUN legs = ELF-x86_64 + arm64-macho: the ELF walker emits a DT_NEEDED for libz.so.1 (the D-FFI-MATH-LIBM-DT-NEEDED per-referenced-library mechanism), so the Linux WSL run-green leg + ubuntu CI link and run it; the Mach-O walker now AUTO-EMITS an LC_LOAD_DYLIB for the non-libSystem import library /usr/lib/libz.1.dylib (D-FFI-MACHO-NONDEFAULT-DYLIB-LOAD, the Mach-O sibling of the ELF DT_NEEDED walker), so the macos-latest (Apple Silicon) CI leg links a native arm64 Mach-O against the system libz and RUNS it to exit 42 through dyld (a byte-perfect Mach-O can still fail to load, so only the run proves the LC_LOAD_DYLIB + binds are correct end-to-end). NOT arm64-elf (no aarch64 libz in the /usr/aarch64-linux-gnu qemu sysroot -> a run arm would false-red the WSL gate). The DESCRIPTOR is target-agnostic regardless (arm64-elf COMPILE-verify: the front-end accepts zipfile.c+zlib.json and binds the zlib symbols to libz). Release arm runs the optimizer over the (unfoldable) libz calls. RED-ON-DISABLE: delete zlib.json -> undeclared -> compile fails; a broken/unexported import would fail loud at load (127), never silently.
